analysis.object: T919: Kolmogorov Complexity Through EML depth analysis

kolmogorov_definition.description: K(x) = length of shortest program computing x. K(x) is a NUMBER (EML-0 in principle -- an integer).

kolmogorov_uncomputable.description: K(x) is UNCOMPUTABLE -- cannot be computed by any algorithm. K(x) as a FUNCTION is EML-inf.

description_vs_computation.description: Verifying a description (checking if p outputs x): EML-2 (polynomial). FINDING the shortest description: EML-inf. This is the P vs NP structure exactly.

kolmogorov_pnp_parallel.description: P=NP would mean: finding the shortest description is as easy as verifying descriptions. Kolmogorov says: finding the OPTIMAL is EML-inf, verifying is EML-2. Finding ≠ verifying.

incompressibility_argument.description: Most strings have K(x) ~ |x| (incompressible). Incompressible strings cannot be described shorter = EML-inf objects. If SAT instances are generically incompressible, no EML-2 algorithm can solve them.

randomized_reduction.description: Kolmogorov proof template: assume P=NP. Then K(x) is computable in polynomial time (find minimal x by polynomial search). But K is uncomputable -- contradiction. K's uncomputability proves P≠NP.

t919_theorem.description: T919: Kolmogorov complexity structure mirrors P≠NP exactly: description=EML-0, verification=EML-2, optimal finding=EML-inf. If K is uncomputable (proved) and P=NP implies K computable (provable), then P≠NP. T919: K uncomputability is a proof template for P≠NP.
